exploration_log.aha_moment: The key block at lines 273-280 already has the two cases: when AllowHeaders is set vs when it's empty. I just needed to add validation logic inside the first case when there is a request header to validate against.
exploration_log.hindsight: I would go directly to the preflight handling block (after line 253) and focus on the allowHeaders check.

alternatives: Validate headers before setting any CORS response headers
Validate only in the allowHeaders set branch
key_decisions.decision: Validate only when allowHeaders is non-empty and there is a request header to check
key_decisions.reasoning: When AllowHeaders is empty the middleware echoes back whatever was requested (permissive fallback). When AllowHeaders is set, we enforce it. No request header means nothing to validate.

solve_notes: Modified `ToMiddleware()` in `middleware/cors.go` to validate `Access-Control-Request-Headers` against configured `AllowHeaders` when specific headers are configured. When a preflight request includes headers that are not in `AllowHeaders`, the `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` response header is omitted, causing the browser's CORS check to fail.
task_text:
The CORS middleware in `middleware/cors.go` accepts preflight requests
and responds with configured `AllowHeaders` regardless of whether the
headers listed in `Access-Control-Request-Headers` are a subset of the
configured `AllowHeaders`. When specific `AllowHeaders` are configured,
the middleware should split and trim the comma-separated
`Access-Control-Request-Headers` value, check each header against the
allowed list (case-insensitively), and omit the
`Access-Control-Allow-Headers` response header (causing the browser to
fail the CORS check) when the requested headers are not a subset. Fix
`CORSConfig.ToMiddleware()` to perform this server-side validation.

thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: an AI agent would read the CORSConfig struct to understand all config fields including AllowHeaders before implementing the validation

tier_difference_reasoning: CORSConfig struct is in thrash_preventing because an AI agent would read the full config struct to understand the AllowHeaders field type ([]string) before implementing the validation logic. Without it, the agent might assume AllowHeaders is a string instead of a slice.
